message:
🐛 fix: wait for state flush in staleness drop test (#8285) (#8287)

The cache-staleness test asserted token/user were null immediately after
isLoading flipped false, but the drop-to-login path sets isLoading=false
first and then fires setTokenState(null)/setUser(null) in the failed-fetch
branch. React batches those into a second render, so the direct expect
saw the still-populated token.

Move both assertions into the waitFor block so vitest waits for the full
state flush, matching how the happy-path tests already gate on the
post-fetch render.

Fixes #8285

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -693,11 +693,13 @@ describe('cached user staleness bound (#6067)', () => {
     vi.stubGlobal('fetch', mockFetch)
 
     const { result } = await renderWithAuthProvider()
-    await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false))
-
-    // Stale cache → session dropped
-    expect(result.current.token).toBeNull()
-    expect(result.current.user).toBeNull()
+    // Stale cache + failed refetch fires setTokenState(null)/setUser(null) —
+    // wait for both flushes, not just the isLoading flip.
+    await waitFor(() => {
+      expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false)
+      expect(result.current.token).toBeNull()
+      expect(result.current.user).toBeNull()
+    })
   })
 
   it('writes validated timestamp after successful /api/me fetch', async () => {
